title: Dontostemon integrifolius
taxonomy: Dontostemon integrifolius is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is also known as the Chinese giant mustard or Chinese horse radish. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants in the same family. The plant's classification helps in understanding its evolution, behavior, and interactions with its environment. The classification of Dontostemon integrifolius as a member of the Brassicaceae family highlights its close relationships with other cruciferous plants.

intro: Dontostemon integrifolius, also known as Chinese angelica or Baishanzhe, is a perennial plant species in the Brassicaceae family native to China. It has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including inflammation, fever, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its potential health benefits such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties.
